title: Coleus goetzenii
intro: Coleus goetzenii, also known as Coleus barbatus or Coleus goetzenii, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to tropical Africa and widely cultivated for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, headache, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coleus goetzenii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, such as genkwanin and rosmarinic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Coleus goetzenii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Coleus goetzenii is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, which is commonly known as the mint or deadnettle family. This classification places Coleus goetzenii among the numerous flowering plants with aromatic, square stems. Coleus goetzenii is part of the genus Coleus, which is a group of tropical plants native to Africa and Asia. The classification of Coleus goetzenii as a member of the Lamiaceae family indicates its close relation to plants such as mint, basil, and oregano. Further classification at the species level places Coleus goetzenii within its own unique species.
